subject: Add DroolsExecutor for DroolsRunnable
commit_message:
Add DroolsExecutor for DroolsRunnable

Added DroolsExecutor, which is an Executor that works by injecting
its commands into drools working memory via DroolsRunnable objects.

Issue-ID: POLICY-2385
Signed-off-by: Jim Hahn <jrh3@att.com>
Change-Id: I59a57bc431249c84cb08f87089f6239993d7d2c4

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

How did you get this to finally verify? Luck I think - I have a review up that reverts the changes I made earlier today.
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Not sure why we need this class Jim, provided that there is an executor in the PolicyEngine.  In general, I am not sure I like the proliferation of executor in any place, and then dealing with the canceling of the tasks, etc .. in shutdown places from multiple places

Patch Set 3:

> Not sure why we need this class Jim, provided that there is an
 > executor in the PolicyEngine.  In general, I am not sure I like the
 > proliferation of executor in any place, and then dealing with the
 > canceling of the tasks, etc .. in shutdown places from multiple
 > places

The purpose of this class is to execute tasks within the drools engine (i.e., KieSession, not PolicyEngine).  Drools-pdp won't use it, but drools-apps will create instances and pass them to the Actors as an "Executor" so that when the Actors invoke callbacks, the callbacks will be executed within the drools engine.  The executor contained within PolicyEngine is for asynchronous tasks and does not execute them within the drools engine.  I could move this class to drools-apps, but it kind of makes sense as a general-purpose utility since it's closely tied to DroolsRunnable, which is also in drools-pdp/core.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

 > this class to drools-apps, but it kind of makes sense as a
 > general-purpose utility since it's closely tied to DroolsRunnable,
 > which is also in drools-pdp/core.

Thanks for the feedback, I think it is ok here then.   It always bugged me (this is previous to your work) that the actors were doing manipulation of the working memory, but may be there is no way to work around it (?).   One issue if the actors depend on drools WorkingMemory that I wonder, if it imposes restrictions to be used outside of drools for example in apex.
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2

Patch Set 3:

> > > Not sure why we need this class Jim, provided that there is an
 > > > executor in the PolicyEngine.  In general, I am not sure I like
 > > the
 > > > proliferation of executor in any place, and then dealing with
 > the
 > > > canceling of the tasks, etc .. in shutdown places from multiple
 > > > places
 > >
 > > The purpose of this class is to execute tasks within the drools
 > > engine (i.e., KieSession, not PolicyEngine).  Drools-pdp won't
 > use
 > > it, but drools-apps will create instances and pass them to the
 > > Actors as an "Executor" so that when the Actors invoke callbacks,
 > > the callbacks will be executed within the drools engine.  The
 > > executor contained within PolicyEngine is for asynchronous tasks
 > > and does not execute them within the drools engine.  I could move
 > > this class to drools-apps, but it kind of makes sense as a
 > > general-purpose utility since it's closely tied to
 > DroolsRunnable,
 > > which is also in drools-pdp/core.
 > 
 > Thanks for the feedback, I think it is ok here then.   It always
 > bugged me (this is previous to your work) that the actors were
 > doing manipulation of the working memory, but may be there is no
 > way to work around it (?).   One issue if the actors depend on
 > drools WorkingMemory that I wonder, if it imposes restrictions to
 > be used outside of drools for example in apex.

In this case, as far as the actors know, they are just submitting tasks to an executor.  It's actually the invoker, in this case drools-apps event manager, that will provide this executor to the actors.  On the other hand, if drools-apps event manager is thread safe, then it can provide the default executor (ForkJoinPool) to the actors.  Either way, the actors, themselves, know nothing about working memory.
